channel.title: Mach Five Magnet
channel.description: Recent content on Mach Five Magnet

item.title: Introduction
item.description: What is Mach Five Magnet? Mach Five Magnet is conversational lead capture for your website. Instead of a static form, you embed a magnet: a chat bubble, slide-in panel, pop-up modal, or inline card that greets the visitor, offers them a channel, and walks them through a short guided conversation. When they finish, you get a lead: every answer, plus the campaign that brought them. You build and manage magnets in the Mach Five Magnet app.

item.title: Quickstart
item.description: Step 1: Sign up Create an account at machfivemagnet.com/app/#/signup, or log in if you already have one. Step 2: Create a magnet Go to Magnets and click New magnet. Name it, something like “Homepage quote magnet”. Enter the domain(s) it should run on, comma-separated (e.g. example.com, www.example.com). Pick a display format: chat bubble, slide-in panel, modal, or inline. Note The domain list is an allow-list, enforced server-side: the magnet only loads on domains you list here.

item.title: Concepts
item.description: Magnets A magnet is one embeddable conversational widget: a display format, an appearance, a welcome hub, and one or more flows. You create and edit magnets in the app; the embed script delivers them to your site. A page renders one magnet per display format, so a chat bubble and an inline card can coexist on the same page without touching each other. Each magnet has: A domain allow-list: the script is only served to domains you list, enforced server-side.

item.title: Integrations & data
item.description: The embed is the integration Mach Five Magnet doesn’t ask your site to talk to an API. The integration is the snippet itself: <script src="https://machfivemagnet-saas.onrender.com/m5t/v5/coreSnippet?appguid=YOUR-MAGNET-ID" async></script> When a visitor loads your page, the platform serves that script with your magnet’s current configuration (appearance, welcome hub, flows, A/B variants) already inside it. Save a change in the app and the next page load has it. There are no API keys on the page, no build step, and nothing to redeploy.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Questions about pricing, agencies, or getting a magnet on your site? Start the conversation below. Prefer email? hello@machfivemagnet.com.

item.title: Magnet for B2B Lead Gen
item.description: Long forms kill conversion. Short forms miss context. You know the tradeoff. A long form gets you qualified leads but tanks your conversion rate. A short form gets volume but buries your team in unqualified noise. Magnet eliminates the tradeoff. A conversational flow collects the same information as a 10-field form, but each question builds on the last. It feels fast because it’s relevant. The prospect doesn’t think “I’m filling out a form.

item.title: Magnet for B2B SaaS
item.description: The problem with SaaS demo forms Your visitor clicks “Book a Demo.” They see a form with 8 fields. Name, email, company, role, company size, use case, timeline, budget. They fill in three and leave. You never hear from them. Magnet replaces that form with a quick conversation. Two or three questions, branched based on their answers, and they’re looking at a calendar. The whole thing takes 30 seconds. How SaaS teams use Magnet Demo booking flows that qualify while they convert.

item.title: Magnet for E-Commerce
item.description: Shoppers don’t want to search. They want to be shown. Product catalogs are overwhelming. Filters help, but most visitors don’t know what they’re filtering for. A product quiz asks three questions and recommends the right item. It feels like talking to a knowledgeable associate, not using a search engine. How e-commerce stores use Magnet Product recommendation quizzes that guide shoppers to the right item. “What’s the occasion?” “What’s your budget?” “Do you prefer classic or modern?

item.title: Magnet for Professional Services
item.description: Your contact form doesn’t tell you anything useful “Name, email, message.” That’s what most service firm contact pages collect. Then someone on your team has to email back, ask qualifying questions, go back and forth, and eventually schedule a call. That’s three days of friction for what could be a 30-second conversation. How service firms use Magnet Consultation booking flows that collect context before the meeting. What do they need help with?
